Add nft-manager: nftables firewall manager for Proxmox hosts

TUI (Textual) + CLI sharing a common core library.

Core modules:
- config.py: TOML-based config with dataclasses, snapshot management
- proxmox.py: httpx API client for VM/CT discovery
- generator.py: deterministic nftables ruleset generation
- validator.py: conflict detection, syntax checking, 9 health checks
- applier.py: safe apply with rollback watchdog and connectivity probe
- state.py: three-way reconciliation (config / Proxmox / live nft)

CLI: argparse with subcommands, JSON output, --dry-run, --no-api
TUI: 8 screens (Dashboard, IP Pool, VMs, Forwards, Lockdown,
     Blacklist, Logs, Config Preview)

Supports public IP, shared NAT, and dedicated NAT VM modes.
All network-specific values are user-configured (no hardcoded IPs).
Config files written with 0600 permissions.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add Regression & Functional Verification section to agent rules (#2)

Agents were only required to run unit tests, which missed regressions
in user-facing flows (e.g., auth working but dashboard inaccessible
post-login). This new subsection under Testing requires agents to
identify critical user paths before coding and verify all of them
after changes — not just the path they touched. Includes a checklist
for web apps (auth, authenticated pages, integrations, navigation,
API endpoints) and rules for checking logs, testing multiple user
states, and flagging untested flows in context.md.

Co-authored-by: npezarro <npezarro@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix session tree: remove false date-based grouping (#744)

* data: persist session cache

* Fix session tree: remove false date-based grouping (Step 1b)

Step 1b forced all sessions on the same date under one root,
incorrectly classifying independent sessions as continuations.
Session #733 was marked 🔁 instead of 💬 because it shared a
date with #675. Now only explicit related_issues links determine
parent/child relationships.

https://claude.ai/code/session_01J19LiYNrAA7iENmtxyeDVJ

---------

New component that makes the site feel dynamic and data-driven:

1. Fee Change Alerts — shows recent platform fee changes from

2. Trending Comparisons — shows popular head-to-head matchups

Placed between comparison table and email capture on homepage.

Add Discord stop hook for posting agent turns to Discord (#3)

The WordPress stop hook posts every Claude turn as a blog draft.
This adds a parallel Discord hook that posts the same turns as
embeds in #claude-agent-logs via webhook. Includes the same
redaction patterns as the WordPress hook plus Discord-specific
token patterns. Also updates agent.md to reflect dual-posting
awareness so future agents know their output goes to both
WordPress and Discord.

Co-authored-by: npezarro <npezarro@users.noreply.github.com>
Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: prevent card scroll handler from intercepting button clicks

The card click handler used e.target.tagName === 'BUTTON' which missed
clicks on child elements (SVG icons, spans) inside buttons. This caused
the scroll-to-highlight handler to fire when clicking Apply/Resolve/
Delete, sometimes scrolling the document unexpectedly. Now uses
e.target.closest() to check the full ancestor chain.

* fix: reduce false positives for OTP/verification-code SMS

Adds OtpDetector which classifies messages as safe when they contain
a 4-8 digit OTP code + a verification keyword and have no URL, no
payment/threat keywords, and no sensitive data requests.

SmsEventProcessor now short-circuits before risk scoring for safe OTP
messages: they are neither persisted nor notified.

OTP + URL / payment threat / sensitive data still runs normal scoring
and can trigger alerts as before.

commit.message:
fix: add missing audit constants for household phone sync

Fix daily script failure error:
ReferenceError: AUDIT_HOUSEHOLD_PHONE_SYNC_FAILED is not defined

Root cause: syncHouseholdPhonesFromPrimary() function in NotificationService.js
references 3 audit constants that were not defined in Config.js:
- AUDIT_HOUSEHOLD_PHONE_SYNC
- AUDIT_HOUSEHOLD_PHONE_SYNC_SKIPPED
- AUDIT_HOUSEHOLD_PHONE_SYNC_COMPLETE
- AUDIT_HOUSEHOLD_PHONE_SYNC_FAILED (the reported error)

Solution: Add all 4 constants to Config.js in the AUDIT constants section.

This nightly task (runs at 2:40 AM SAST) syncs household phone numbers from
primary member to household record. The task will now complete successfully
without throwing ReferenceError.

Note: Feature is controlled by HOUSEHOLD_PHONE_SYNC_ENABLED config variable
(currently set to true).

Co-Authored-By: Claude Haiku 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

chore: add workflow rule to always open PR after implementation (#25)

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: RetroArch-style audio resampler eliminates Android crackles (#48)

Replace direct AudioTrack writes at the core's native sample rate with a
RetroArch-inspired audio pipeline:

  core samples (e.g. 32029 Hz) → linear interpolation resampler → AudioTrack (48000 Hz)

The resampler uses dynamic rate control (±0.5% based on buffer fill level)
to compensate for clock drift between the system clock and the audio
hardware clock, keeping the buffer at ~50% full. The ±0.5% pitch change
is inaudible (<9 cents).

This fixes persistent audio crackles and pops on Android that were caused
by the mismatch between the core's native sample rate and Android's
internal resampling. By resampling to 48000 Hz ourselves, we bypass
Android's internal resampler and incorporate drift compensation directly
into the resampling math.

Frame pacing now uses precisionSleep instead of relying on blocking audio
writes, decoupling frame timing from audio output.

Tested on SNES, Genesis, and GameCube — all sound clean with stable 60 FPS.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

commit.message:
Add all-in-one notebook: setup + pipeline combined

- Combines local_colab_setup and example_pipeline
- Run all cells from top to bottom
- Setup (Part 1) + Pipeline (Part 2) in one place
- Easier for beginners - no need to manage multiple notebooks

Usage:
1. Open all_in_one.ipynb in Colab
2. Run all cells sequentially
3. Setup happens automatically, then pipeline runs

Benefits:
- Single file workflow
- Clear separation (Part 1: Setup, Part 2: Pipeline)
- Can re-run pipeline part without re-setup
- Test query + Gradio demo included

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix(export): sanitize CSS class names starting with digits in preview

CSS identifiers cannot start with a digit — `.333` is invalid and silently
ignored by browsers. Added `safeCSSName()` that prefixes such names with `_`.

Also includes: UNITLESS_PROPS for opacity/lineHeight/zIndex, blendMode export,
Webflow-model class handling, breakpoint position/fills overrides, CSS reset
for img/button/input elements.

Add Next.js web app: March Madness bracket predictor UI

- Full-stack Next.js 16 + Tailwind CSS 4 web app in web/
- Dark basketball theme (navy + orange) with Geist font
- Home page: hero, animated matchup predictor (pick any two 2024 tournament
  teams, see ML win probability with animated bar and count-up animation)
- Bracket page: 4 regional brackets with round-by-round game slots showing
  predicted winner, actual result (✓/✗), and model accuracy summary
- API routes: /api/predict (team pair → win prob), /api/bracket (full data)
- Data prep script: web/scripts/prepare_bracket.py generates bracket.json
  from submission.csv + tournament seeds + actual results
- Mobile-responsive nav with hamburger menu
- Clean build (npm run build passes, 0 TypeScript errors)

Guard cloud ray intersection against near-horizontal rays

Clamp ray_dir.y > 0.01 and cap t_cloud at 10000 to prevent precision
issues (banding/shimmer) from extremely large UV coordinates when rays
graze the horizon.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

fix: Security hardening — 403 info leak, 500 on trial users, payment spam

- require_plan: strip current/required plan from 403 response (info disclosure)
- is_trial_active() + get_subscription: handle naive DB datetimes vs aware now()
  (PostgreSQL DateTime strips timezone → TypeError on comparison → 500 for free users)
- mark_user_paid: add per-reference attempt counter (max 3, 1h TTL via cache)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Sonnet 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix 56 broken featured images caused by case-sensitive filenames

MDX frontmatter referenced Title-Case filenames (e.g. How-to-Sell-Tour-Packages.webp)
but git tracks them as lowercase (how-to-sell-tour-packages.webp). Vercel runs Linux
which is case-sensitive, so these returned 404. Updated all MDX files to use the
correct lowercase filenames.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix propose-beliefs: filter existing, add --entry flag, fix path resolution

Three improvements from real-world testing feedback:

1. propose-beliefs now reads beliefs.md and filters out already-accepted
   belief IDs from the output. Also passes existing IDs to the LLM prompt
   so it avoids proposing semantic duplicates.

2. New --entry flag: `propose-beliefs --entry entries/2026/03/04/foo.md`
   processes a single entry instead of re-processing all entries.
   Supports multiple --entry flags.

3. explain file now resolves paths relative to the target repo root,
   not just cwd. Fixes failures when running from the expert repo
   directory with paths like `langflow_components/foo.py`.

commit.message:
feat: geocode SQLite FTS5 pipeline Step 2 (issue #8)

Add scripts/build-geocode-db.py that parses OSM nodes with name or
addr:street tags using osmium Python bindings and builds a SQLite FTS5
database for address search. Replace the Step 2 placeholder in
build-region.sh with osmium tags-filter, merge, Python conversion, and
R2 upload. Add osmium and python3 to required tools check. Document
Step 2 and prerequisites in scripts/README.md.

Co-authored-by: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

Fix batch overflow hang and add comprehensive inference logging

The app would hang around the 4th-5th message because all prompt tokens
were stuffed into a single 512-token batch buffer, causing a buffer
overwrite. Fixed by chunking prefill into batch-sized pieces. Also added
context window overflow protection (auto-truncates old messages) and
os.log logging throughout the inference and chat pipeline.

commit.message:
fix(security): close 16 multi-tenancy read-path vulnerabilities (#75)

* fix(security): close 16 multi-tenancy read-path vulnerabilities

- 5 getFundsFor* helpers now enforce role-based fund scoping
- getCapitalCalls/getDistributions WHERE clauses include fundId
- getCapitalCall/getDistribution single-record fetches verify fund access
- getCapitalCallPDFData/getDistributionPDFData verify fund access
- getCapitalCallSummary/getDistributionSummary scoped to active fund
- getLPChartData scoped to active fund (was cross-fund — security risk)
- Document download/upload routes verify investor fund boundary
- 8 negative security tests for cross-fund access denial

Closes 16 read-path vulnerabilities. No write-path changes.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

* fix(security): address code review findings on PR #75

- Document download: add default-deny else clause for orphaned documents
- Document routes: use ...notDeleted convention instead of inline deletedAt
- Capital call/distribution single-record fetches: add console.error to
  catch blocks per CLAUDE.md audit/observability requirement
- Capital call/distribution single-record fetches: resolve currency from
  the record's fund, not the active fund (fixes cross-fund formatting)
- Capital call/distribution list queries: parse pagination params before
  early returns to preserve caller's page/pageSize in empty responses
